title: Keeping His First Wife Now
content:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ It's oh! for a rivet in marriage bonds,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And a splice in the knot untied,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The sanctity of the marriage tie
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Is growing more sanctified!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Theyן¿½re getting mixed up in society,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Thereן¿½s an awful family row,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ For Reginald Jones of ן¿½The Ferneryן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Is ן¿½keepingן¿½ his first wife now!

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Oh! she belonged to the smart, smart set
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ (Where reasons are far to seek),
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And the wedding and ן¿½crushן¿½ are remembered yet
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ As the ן¿½smartן¿½ things of the week.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Never an atom of love had she,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ But they had a child somehow,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And Reginald Jones of ן¿½The Ferneryן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Has the love of his first wife now.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Mad for ן¿½noticeן¿½ and ן¿½talkן¿½ was she,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A butterfly blind as a bat,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ She would flaunt for a season a divorcee,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Or divorce him, failing that.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ He played his part and she held his heart
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ As light as her marriage vow,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ But Reginald Jones of ן¿½The Ferneryן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Has a hold on his first wife now.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ She swore in Court what the world knew false,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ With never a thought of shame,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ She was free to flaunt to her heartן¿½s content,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ But she found it mighty tame:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The talk of the ן¿½townן¿½ for a week or two,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The gush, the smirk and the bow,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ But Reginald Jones of ן¿½The Ferneryן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Is the God of his first wife now!

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Her soul grew sick of the smart, smart set,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Or her conscience drove her wild,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Or she craved for ן¿½noticeן¿½ and ן¿½talkן¿½ once more,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Or perhaps because of the child;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ But they met at last and they met again,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ No matter the where or how,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And Reginald Jones of ן¿½The Ferneryן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Is in love with his first wife now.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Tis a ן¿½terrible lifeן¿½ for the second young wife,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ But she married him too for ן¿½placeן¿½;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And she mustnן¿½t forget that a smarting set
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Belongs to the human race.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ They say itן¿½s fixed up in camera,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And, if that is the case, Iן¿½ll vow,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ That Reginald Jones of ן¿½The Ferneryן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Will marry his first wife now.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And there is a song of the English world
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And a song for the English race:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The second husband and second wife
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Must ever take second place.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ So cherish the best that you find in the first,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And a margin of width allow:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The future looks after itself too well!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Look after the first ones now.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Henry Lawson represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Henry Lawson's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
